name: Claude Managed Agents + ant CLI
summary: public beta for building and deploying Claude agents — sandboxed execution, checkpointing, scoped permissions, SSE streaming; new ant CLI lets you ship agents from the terminal

name: Meta Muse Spark
summary: Meta's first closed-source model — voice/text/image input, live on meta.ai now. Can generate minigames, excels at visual STEM, rolling out to WhatsApp and Ray-Ban glasses

name: GLM-5.1
summary: MIT-licensed open 744B MoE model (40B active, 200K context) — topped SWE-Bench for coding, first open model to beat all closed rivals on real-world code; weights on Hugging Face

name: Claude Cowork + Dispatch
summary: Claude desktop control now on Windows — clicks, types, opens apps autonomously. Dispatch lets you hand off tasks from your phone while Claude works on your computer

name: Llama 4 Scout + Maverick
summary: Meta's first natively multimodal open models — Scout (17B active/109B total MoE, runs on a single H100) and Maverick (17B active/400B total, beats GPT-4o on most benchmarks); video and image understanding, Apache 2.0

name: Cursor 3
summary: rebuilt IDE around parallel AI agents — run many agents at once across local, cloud, worktree, and remote SSH; new Design Mode lets you annotate UI elements directly in the browser

name: Google Gemma 4
summary: four open-weight models (2B–31B) with Apache 2.0 license — runs on a Raspberry Pi or Mac Mini, built from Gemini 3 research, most permissive Gemma release yet

name: Veo 3.1 Lite
summary: accessible text-to-video and image-to-video model — Veo 3.1 Fast pricing slashed to open up developer access for solo creators and small teams

name: GPT-4.1
summary: instruction-following and long-context improvements over GPT-4o — 1M token context window, reduced hallucinations, and significantly cheaper API pricing aimed at developers

name: Gemma 3
summary: open-weight family from 1B to 27B parameters — multimodal, 128K context, runs locally on a single GPU; tops Llama 3.3 70B on benchmarks at fraction of the size

name: GPT-4.5
summary: OpenAI's largest and most emotionally intelligent model yet — trained with more compute than any previous GPT, better at following nuanced instructions and open-ended conversation

name: Claude 3.7 Sonnet
summary: first model with extended thinking — can spend up to 128K tokens reasoning before answering; best on coding and math of any Claude at launch, sets new bar for agentic tasks

name: o3-mini
summary: fast reasoning model at low cost — three effort levels (low/medium/high), beats o1 on AIME math and SWE-Bench coding while running significantly faster and cheaper

name: DeepSeek R1
summary: open-weight reasoning model trained with reinforcement learning — matches o1 on math and coding at a fraction of the cost; MIT licensed, weights freely available

name: DeepSeek V3
summary: 671B MoE model (37B active) trained on 14.8T tokens for $5.5M — matches or beats GPT-4o and Claude 3.5 Sonnet on most benchmarks; MIT licensed weights on Hugging Face

name: Gemini 2.0 Flash
summary: next-gen workhorse model from Google — twice the speed of 1.5 Flash, native image and audio output, live API for real-time multimodal streaming; marks the agentic era shift

name: Claude 3.5 Haiku
summary: fastest model in the Claude 3.5 family — matches Claude 3 Opus on many tasks, designed for high-volume agentic use cases at significantly lower cost

name: Claude Computer Use
summary: Claude can now see your screen and control mouse and keyboard — first AI to take direct computer actions; public beta in the API with demo agent on GitHub

name: o1 Preview
summary: first OpenAI model to reason before answering — trained with RL to chain-of-thought privately before responding; PhD-level performance on physics/chemistry/bio evals

name: Grok 2
summary: xAI's flagship model with real-time X data access — image generation via FLUX, performance competitive with GPT-4o and Claude 3.5 Sonnet on standard benchmarks

name: Llama 3.1 (405B)
summary: Meta's first frontier-class open model at 405B parameters — first to surpass GPT-4 class performance on many benchmarks; 128K context, multilingual, Apache 2.0 license

name: Mistral Large 2
summary: 123B model with 128K context and strong code/math performance — beats Llama 3 405B on reasoning, natively multilingual, available under Mistral Research License

name: GPT-4o mini
summary: small model that replaced GPT-3.5 Turbo as the default — smarter than GPT-4 Turbo at launch, costs 60% less than GPT-3.5, vision built in, target is high-volume tasks

name: Claude 3.5 Sonnet
summary: sat at #1 on most benchmarks for months after launch — 2× the speed of Claude 3 Opus at 1/5 the cost, new Artifacts feature for inline code preview, introduced Artifacts UI

name: GPT-4o
summary: first truly multimodal model — processes text, audio, and images in a single model (not separate pipelines); real-time voice mode with emotional tone, 2× faster than GPT-4 Turbo

name: Llama 3 (8B + 70B)
summary: Meta's most capable open models to date — 8B matches GPT-3.5, 70B competitive with GPT-4 on many benchmarks; trained on 15T tokens, commercial license for most uses

name: Claude 3 (Haiku, Sonnet, Opus)
summary: three-tier model family — Opus topped GPT-4 on most evals at launch, Sonnet matched GPT-4 at half the cost, Haiku the fastest small model on the market; 200K context across all three

name: Gemini 1.5 Pro
summary: breakthrough 1M token context window — can process an hour of video, 700K words, or 30K lines of code in a single prompt; MoE architecture allows near-GPT-4 performance

name: Sora
summary: text-to-video model generating minute-long clips with realistic physics and camera movements — debuted as a research preview; demonstrated characters, scenes, and multiple shots in one take
